channel.title: Agent Nouveau — Facebook
channel.description: Agent Nouveau journal — Facebook editorial feed

item.title: Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
We're watching Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch, the Edwardian landmark on The Mall that, for more than a century, was only ever something you passed through.
Sir Aston Webb designed it, the same hand behind the Buckingham Palace façade, and King Edward VII raised it in memory of Queen Victoria. No guest has ever spent a night inside. That changes in autumn 2026... reservations opened in June for a first-ever hotel of 114 rooms and suites, naval names like the Nelson and the Victory, dining from Clare Smyth and Daniel Boulud, and a spa set into the old stonework opposite the Palace.
A door the whole city knows, opening inward for the first time. Some landmarks are worth the wait to sleep in.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the rooms history kept locked until now. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #WaldorfAstoria #AdmiraltyArch #London #TheMall #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Comporta, Alentejo (Portugal)
item.description:
Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
We're dreaming of Comporta this morning... the stretch of Alentejo an hour south of Lisbon where the rice fields, not the resorts, still set the tone.
Imagine a place the Lisbon set has guarded since the 1950s and never quite told anyone about: low white houses under umbrella pines, wild Atlantic beaches with no one on them, storks nesting over the Sado estuary, and the stilt cabins of Carrasqueira leaning out over the tide. There are no high-rises, no strip of signage, no scene to perform for. The dress code is bare feet. Even now, with Sublime's new villas drawing a wider crowd, Comporta keeps its trick of making everyone pretend not to notice each other.
The Algarve gets the postcards. Comporta gets the people who would rather you didn't know.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the places that stay quiet on purpose. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Comporta #Alentejo #Portugal #SecretEscape #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
We're watching Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch, the Edwardian landmark on The Mall that, for more than a century, was only ever something you passed through.
Sir Aston Webb designed it, the same hand behind the Buckingham Palace façade, and King Edward VII raised it in memory of Queen Victoria. No guest has ever spent a night inside. That changes in autumn 2026... reservations opened in June for a first-ever hotel of 114 rooms and suites, naval names like the Nelson and the Victory, dining from Clare Smyth and Daniel Boulud, and a spa set into the old stonework opposite the Palace.
A door the whole city knows, opening inward for the first time. Some landmarks are worth the wait to sleep in.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the rooms history kept locked until now. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #WaldorfAstoria #AdmiraltyArch #London #TheMall #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
item.description:
Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
We're dreaming of Comporta this morning... the stretch of Alentejo an hour south of Lisbon where the rice fields, not the resorts, still set the tone.
Imagine a place the Lisbon set has guarded since the 1950s and never quite told anyone about: low white houses under umbrella pines, wild Atlantic beaches with no one on them, storks nesting over the Sado estuary, and the stilt cabins of Carrasqueira leaning out over the tide. There are no high-rises, no strip of signage, no scene to perform for. The dress code is bare feet. Even now, with Sublime's new villas drawing a wider crowd, Comporta keeps its trick of making everyone pretend not to notice each other.
The Algarve gets the postcards. Comporta gets the people who would rather you didn't know.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the places that stay quiet on purpose. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Comporta #Alentejo #Portugal #SecretEscape #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: ⟡ More than half of travellers would now plan a trip around food.
item.description:
⟡ More than half of travellers would now plan a trip around food.
In Booking.com's 2026 predictions, 55 per cent said they would consider going somewhere chosen for what it puts on the table.
The figure is new. The instinct behind it is not. For years the destination came first and the meal was a happy accident found on the second evening. Now the meal often comes first… the table booked, the flight arranged around it.
We rather like that order.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The best tables open before anything else does.
item.description:
⟡ The best tables open before anything else does.
A particular dining room releases its reservations weeks, sometimes months, ahead. So the table gets booked first, because the table is the thing that cannot be moved.
Everything else arranges itself around that one fixed point. The nights either side. The walk you take to work up an appetite. The slow morning after.
A meal is a good reason to go somewhere. It has a date and a time and a place, which is more than most holidays begin with. 🖤
Join us, in great company. 🕶️

item.title: ⟡ One table, not ten.
item.description:
⟡ One table, not ten.
We are not talking about the food tour, the kind that turns a city into a checklist of plates to tick off before the flight home.
What we notice is quieter. One dinner that matters. A long lunch in a place someone has read about for years. A table by a window in a town they would not otherwise have visited… chosen because the meal is worth the journey, and the journey turns out to be worth taking for its own sake.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Table With Ten Seats
item.description:
⟡ The Table With Ten Seats
The Westbourne Arcade has stood on Poole Road since 1885, a covered street built so people could walk through and meet one another. A hundred and forty years on, that is still rather what Westbourne is for.
From our corner of it at No.82, we have been thinking about how much business now happens at a distance, on a screen, between people who have never shared a table. We use it like everyone else. But it is not the same as an hour across the table from someone, and we do not think the two are interchangeable.
Our new journal piece on Westbourne, in person, and the case for the smallest table.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Ten seats, on purpose
item.description:
⟡ Ten seats, on purpose
A few times now we have set a long table at No.82 and asked a small group of local people to come for breakfast. The next one is on the morning of the twenty-third, with Gayle Swaffield of The Feng Shui Essence joining us to talk about how the spaces we work in shape the way we think.
We keep the table small on purpose. Ten seats, no more. A room would look more impressive in a photograph, but a room is not where the good conversations happen. There is no pitch, no sixty-second sell... only a handful of people who can all hear each other, a hot coffee, and an hour that is genuinely useful.
The details are on our journal. The welcome stands either way.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Suite Shaped Like a Boat
item.description:
⟡ The Suite Shaped Like a Boat
The overwater suites at COMO Cocoa Island, in the South Malé Atoll, are built in the shape of the dhoni, the wooden fishing boat that has worked these waters for centuries. You sleep in the shape of the thing that built the place.
We send people here for what that detail implies, not for the brochure version of the Maldives. The real luxury of an island this small is not what there is to do. It is how little you are asked to. The water moves under the floor all day, the reef begins a few steps from the deck, and within a day or two something in you lets go.
Our new journal piece on COMO, the over-water quiet, and the case for doing less.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Water as the ground floor
item.description:
⟡ Water as the ground floor
On most holidays the sea is somewhere you go. At COMO Cocoa Island it is the floor you live on. The villas stand on slim stilts over the lagoon, the water working softly underneath them all day and all night, and the reef begins a few steps down from your own terrace.
There is no transfer to a dive site, no schedule to keep. You go down the ladder and the coral garden is already there, with the rays and the slow turtles the South Malé Atoll is known for. No roads, no cars, no engine note across the island. The loudest thing is usually the water itself.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The case for doing less
item.description:
⟡ The case for doing less
The mistake people bring to the Maldives is the urge to fill it. To dive every morning, book every excursion, treat the stillness as something to get through. You fly a long way only to be as busy as you were before you left.
The better case is the opposite. Let the days go shapeless. Swim when you wake, eat when you are hungry, take the morning yoga or skip it, watch the light change on the water. An island this small does not reward the collector. It rewards the person willing to stop.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A seat at the table ↠ 25 June
item.description:
⟡ A seat at the table ↠ 25 June
The room at No.82 is small, which is the whole idea. A handful of people around good food and better company, an evening of South African food, wine and stories with Marguerite Mornet, and no rush to anywhere.
If a warm South African evening in good company sounds like your kind of night, come find us at No.82 to join us, or write and we will hold you a seat.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A few seats left ↠ South Africa Supper Club
item.description:
⟡ A few seats left ↠ South Africa Supper Club
The room is nearly full for Thursday's evening with Marguerite Mornet, but we have a few seats still to give. An evening of South African food, wine and stories, hosted by someone who knows the country firsthand, with the lights low and the night unhurried.
If you have been meaning to join us, this is the moment to write. We would love to set a place for you.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Tonight at No.82
item.description:
⟡ Tonight at No.82
The table is being laid, the room is warming up, and tonight South Africa comes to Westbourne. An evening of food, wine and stories with Marguerite Mornet, from 6:30pm.
If you are joining us, we will see you shortly. If you meant to and the day got away from you, write quickly... there may still be a seat.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Something South African is coming to No.82
item.description:
⟡ Something South African is coming to No.82
A warm evening is taking shape for later this month. Food, wine and stories from one country, in the company of someone who has spent a lifetime knowing it firsthand.
We are keeping the particulars for the night itself. For now, only this... clear an evening in late June, and watch this space.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The South Africa Supper Club ↠ Thursday 25 June
item.description:
⟡ The South Africa Supper Club ↠ Thursday 25 June
It is an evening of South African food, wine and stories at No.82, hosted by Marguerite Mornet of Africa Travel... a South African specialist who has spent more than thirty years creating safari and wine experiences across the continent.
The room is small by design, the evening unhurried. Expect specialist South African cuisine and wine, and the kind of stories that only come from someone who knows the country firsthand.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82 to join us.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The one that exists nowhere else
item.description:
⟡ The one that exists nowhere else
In 1925 a professor at Stellenbosch named Abraham Perold crossed Pinot Noir with Cinsaut and planted four seeds in his garden. He very nearly lost them. The seedlings were rescued from a clearance by a young lecturer who happened to be passing, and from those vines came Pinotage, a grape that grows seriously almost nowhere on earth but here.
It has not always been easy to love, and that is part of the point. No one argues about a wine with no character. The Cape has a red that belongs to it completely, the way few countries can say of any grape, and the best of it now is very good indeed.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Trip That Earns Its Distance
item.description:
⟡ The Trip That Earns Its Distance
Long-haul destinations grew their share of UK holidays from seven per cent to ten in a single year. Australia, New Zealand, the Pacific Islands... places measured in days of flying, not hours. That figure comes from ABTA's Travel Trends 2026, and it is not a blip.
The far edge of the map is having a quiet revival. Our new Travel Intelligence piece looks at why the considered traveller is choosing one big trip over several small ones, and where that instinct leads.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Fewer trips, more room in each
item.description:
⟡ Fewer trips, more room in each
The 2026 Virtuoso Luxe Report describes travellers slowing down. Lingering longer once they arrive, choosing smaller camps and longer stays over a packed itinerary. Two-thirds of their advisors expect demand to rise next year, and more than half expect clients to spend more per trip.
People are not necessarily travelling more often. They are travelling further, and giving each trip more room. A twenty-four hour journey has never made sense for four nights. It makes a great deal of sense for two unhurried weeks.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The distance is part of the point
item.description:
⟡ The distance is part of the point
South Africa is a long way to go, and that is precisely why it rewards the traveller who stays. A fortnight there, the city and the winelands and the long slow afternoons, returns more than a string of short hops ever could.
The long trip is not an indulgence. It is the better use of the same time and the same money. Further, and then slower once you land... and you come home restored, rather than barely having had time to feel the place at all.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com
